title: Crispy Herbal Filipino Chicken Casserole
description: A low‑fat, solo‑dinner Filipino casserole that balances herbal flavors with a crunchy breadcrumb topping and tiny marshmallows, cooked quickly in a pressure cooker and finished under a broiler.
instructions: In a bowl, combine chicken pieces with minced garlic, ground ginger, turmeric, dried herbs, lemon zest, soy sauce, and vinegar; let marinate 10 minutes.
Preheat the pressure cooker on sauté mode and spray with cooking spray. Add the chicken and sear 2–3 minutes per side until lightly browned. Remove to a plate.
Add diced onion to the cooker; sauté 2 minutes until translucent.
Pour in chicken broth to deglaze the bottom, scraping up any browned bits.
Return the chicken to the cooker, add the miniature marshmallows, and stir gently.
Seal the pressure cooker and cook on high pressure for 8 minutes.
Quick‑release the pressure and open the lid carefully.
Transfer the chicken mixture to an oven‑safe baking dish; sprinkle the panko breadcrumbs evenly over the top.
Broil in the oven at high for 2–3 minutes, or until the breadcrumbs turn golden and crispy. Watch closely to avoid burning.
Serve immediately hot, optionally with a side of steamed greens.
